Navigation: Mifos is a comprehensive microfinance and core banking platform built on Apache Fineract. The application uses a Material Design layout with a left sidebar navigation and a main content area. The login page displays a centered card with fields for Tenant Identifier, Username, and Password along with a Login button. After authentication, the main layout appears with a persistent top toolbar displaying the Mifos logo on the left, a search icon for global search, a notifications bell icon, and the logged-in user's name with a dropdown for profile and logout on the right. The left sidebar contains a collapsible navigation menu organized into logical sections. The primary navigation section includes Dashboard, Clients, Groups, and Centers links. The Products section includes Loan Products, Savings Products, Share Products, Fixed Deposit Products, Recurring Deposit Products, Charges, and Floating Rates. The Accounting section includes Chart of Accounts, Journal Entries, Closing Entries, Accounting Rules, and Financial Activity Mappings. The Organization section includes Offices, Employees, Currencies, Payment Types, Holidays, Working Days, Funds, and Bulk Import. The Admin section includes Users, Roles, Manage Scheduler Jobs, Global Configurations, Manage Codes, Manage Data Tables, Audit Trails, and Account Number Preferences. The Reports section provides access to all configured reports. Each sidebar link navigates to the corresponding page in the main content area. Breadcrumbs appear at the top of the content area showing the navigation path. Entity listing pages display data in Material Design tables with search, filter, sort, and pagination controls. Detail pages display entity information in card layouts with action buttons for state transitions. Forms use Material Design input fields with inline validation.

Functional Overview.1. Login: The login page displays a centered card on a themed background with the Mifos logo at the top. The form contains three fields: Tenant Identifier (text input, pre-filled with "default"), Username (text input with placeholder), and Password (text input, masked). A blue "Login" button spans the bottom of the form. When the user enters valid credentials and clicks Login, the system authenticates via HTTP Basic Authentication against the Fineract backend API, retrieves the user's roles and permissions, and redirects to the Dashboard. If the credentials are invalid, an error message "Invalid authentication details" or "Bad credentials" appears below the form. If any field is left empty, inline validation messages appear (e.g., "Username is required", "Password is required"). The Tenant Identifier field specifies which tenant database to authenticate against in multi-tenant deployments. After successful login, the top toolbar displays the authenticated user's name and the sidebar navigation becomes available with menu items filtered based on the user's assigned role permissions.
Functional Overview.2. Dashboard: The Dashboard is the landing page after login, accessible from the sidebar under the primary navigation section. The page displays key operational metrics and summaries in a card-based layout. The top section shows summary cards for total clients, active loans, pending approvals, and portfolio at risk. A bar chart or line chart displays disbursement trends, collection performance, or portfolio growth over recent months. Below the charts, quick action buttons or links provide shortcuts to frequently used operations such as Create Client, New Loan Application, Make Repayment, and New Savings Account. An activity feed or recent transactions section displays the latest system activities including recent loan approvals, disbursements, and repayments with timestamps and user information. The dashboard content varies based on the user's role and permissions — a loan officer sees their assigned clients and pending tasks, while an administrator sees system-wide metrics.
Functional Overview.3. Global Search: The global search function is accessible by clicking the search icon in the top toolbar from any page. Clicking the icon opens a search bar overlay or a search dialog with a text input field and placeholder text "Search by client name, account number, or ID". As the user types, the system queries across clients, groups, loans, and savings accounts matching the search text. Results appear in a dropdown list below the search bar, categorized by entity type (Clients, Loans, Savings, Groups) with each result showing the entity name, account or ID number, and status. Clicking a result navigates directly to that entity's detail page. If no results are found, a "No results found" message is displayed. The search supports partial matching and is case-insensitive.
Functional Overview.4. Client Management: The Clients page is accessed from the sidebar under the primary navigation section. The page displays a Material Design data table listing all clients in the system with columns for Client Name (clickable link navigating to the client detail page), Account Number, External ID, Status (displayed as a colored chip — Active in green, Pending in yellow, Closed in gray, Rejected in red), Office, and Staff. Above the table, a search/filter bar allows filtering clients by name, account number, office, or status using text inputs and dropdown selectors. A blue floating action button (FAB) with a "+" icon in the bottom-right corner opens the Create Client form. Pagination controls at the bottom show the total count, items per page dropdown, and page navigation arrows. The Create Client form contains fields for Office (dropdown, required), Staff (dropdown), Legal Form (dropdown — Individual or Entity), First Name (text input, required), Middle Name (text input, optional), Last Name (text input, required), Date of Birth (date picker), Gender (dropdown), Mobile Number (text input), Email Address (text input), Submitted On date (date picker, defaults to current date), Activation Date (date picker), and External ID (text input). Additional collapsible sections include Address Details with fields for Address Type, Address Line 1, Address Line 2, City, State, Country, and Postal Code; a Family Members section for adding dependents with Name, Relationship, Gender, Date of Birth, and Profession fields; and an Identifiers section for adding identification documents with Document Type (dropdown — National ID, Passport, Driver License), Document Key, and Description. Required fields show red error text if left empty (e.g., "First name is required", "Office is required"). Upon successful creation, the user is redirected to the new client's detail page. The Client Detail page header displays the client's full name, account number, status badge, activation date, and office name with an image/avatar placeholder. An action button bar provides lifecycle actions based on status: Activate (for Pending clients), Edit, Transfer Client, Assign Staff, Close, Reject, Withdraw, and Reactivate (for Closed clients). The main content is organized into tabbed sections: General (personal information, office, staff assignment), Accounts (sub-tabs for Loan Accounts, Savings Accounts, Share Accounts, and Charges — each showing a table of associated accounts with account number, product name, balance, and status; clicking any account navigates to that account's detail page), Identifiers (list of identification documents with Edit/Delete actions), Family Members, Notes (chronological notes with Add Note button), and Documents (file upload and management). A blue "+" FAB at the bottom-right provides quick actions like New Loan, New Savings, New Share Account, and Add Charge.
Functional Overview.5. Group Management: The Groups page is accessed from the sidebar under the primary navigation section. The page displays a data table listing all groups with columns for Group Name (clickable link), Account Number, Office, Status (Active, Pending, Closed as colored chips), and Staff. A "+" FAB allows creating a new group. The Create Group form contains fields for Office (dropdown, required), Group Name (text input, required), Staff (dropdown), Submitted On date (date picker), External ID (text input), and Activation Date (date picker). A Client Members section with a search and add interface allows associating existing clients to the group — the user types a client name, selects from autocomplete results, and the client is added to the members list with a remove option. Required fields show validation errors if empty (e.g., "Group name is required"). The Group Detail page displays the group name, account number, status, office, and staff at the top with action buttons (Activate, Edit, Close, Assign Staff, Transfer Clients). Below, tabs show Members (list of client members with links to their profiles), Loan Accounts (group loans and GLIM — Group Linked Individual Micro-loans), Savings Accounts (group savings and GSIM accounts), Notes, and a Calendar/Meeting section for scheduling and recording group meetings with columns for meeting date and attendance status. The Collection Sheet feature accessible from a group allows generating a sheet showing all group clients with columns for loan repayment amounts due and savings deposit amounts, enabling batch data entry for field officers collecting payments at group meetings.
Functional Overview.6. Center Management: The Centers page is accessed from the sidebar under the primary navigation section. Centers are higher-level organizational units that contain groups. The page displays a data table listing all centers with columns for Center Name (clickable link), Account Number, Office, Status, and Staff. A "+" FAB opens the Create Center form with fields for Office (dropdown, required), Center Name (text input, required), Staff (dropdown), External ID, Submitted On date, and Activation Date. A Group Members section allows searching and adding existing groups to the center. Required fields show validation errors if empty. The Center Detail page displays the center name, status, office, and staff with action buttons (Activate, Edit, Close, Assign Staff). Tabs below show Groups (list of member groups with links), Loan Accounts, Savings Accounts, Notes, and a Calendar/Meeting section. The Collection Sheet feature accessible from the center generates a comprehensive sheet showing all groups and their clients with columns for loan repayment amounts due and savings deposit amounts, enabling batch data entry for center meeting collections.
Functional Overview.7. Loan Products: The Loan Products page is accessed from the sidebar under the Products section. The page displays a data table listing all configured loan products with columns for Product Name (clickable link), Short Name, and Fund. A "+" FAB opens the Create Loan Product form, which is a comprehensive multi-section form. The Details section includes Product Name (text input, required), Short Name (text input, required, max 4 characters), Description (textarea), Fund (dropdown), Start Date and Close Date (date pickers), Currency (dropdown with code, decimal places, and multiples of rounding), Principal amount with minimum, default, and maximum numeric inputs, Number of Repayments with minimum, default, and maximum, and Repaid Every frequency number with unit dropdown (Days, Weeks, Months). The Settings section includes Amortization method (dropdown — Equal Installments, Equal Principal Payments), Interest Method (dropdown — Flat, Declining Balance), Interest Calculation Period (dropdown — Daily, Same as repayment period), Repayment Strategy (dropdown — Mifos standard, Heavensfamily, Creocore, etc.), Days in Year (dropdown — 360, 365, Actual), Days in Month (dropdown — 30, Actual), and numeric fields for Grace on Principal Payment, Grace on Interest Payment, Grace on Interest Charged, Interest Free Period, and Arrears Tolerance. The Interest Rate section includes Nominal Interest Rate with minimum, default, and maximum fields, and optionally links to Floating Rate definitions. The Charges section allows adding predefined charges/fees with a search-and-add interface. The Accounting section provides radio button selection for None, Cash-based, Accrual (periodic), or Accrual (upfront), and when selected shows dropdown mappings for Fund Source, Loan Portfolio, Interest on Loans, Income from Fees, Income from Penalties, Losses Written Off, and Overpayment Liability GL accounts. All required fields show inline validation errors. A "Submit" button creates the loan product. Clicking an existing product name opens its detail view with an Edit option.
Functional Overview.8. Savings Products: The Savings Products page is accessed from the sidebar under the Products section. The page displays a data table listing all savings products with columns for Product Name (clickable link), Short Name, and Description. Separate pages for Fixed Deposit Products and Recurring Deposit Products are also accessible from the sidebar under Products, each with similar listing and create/edit interfaces. A "+" FAB opens the Create Savings Product form with sections: Details — Product Name (text input, required), Short Name (text input, required, max 4 characters), Description (textarea), Currency (dropdown), Decimal Places, Currency Multiples Of. Terms — Nominal Annual Interest Rate (numeric input), Interest Compounding Period (dropdown — Daily, Monthly, Quarterly, Semi-Annual, Annually), Interest Posting Period (dropdown — Monthly, Quarterly, Biannual, Annually), Interest Calculated Using (dropdown — Daily Balance, Average Daily Balance), Days in Year (dropdown — 360, 365). Settings — Minimum Opening Balance (numeric input), Lock-in Period (numeric input and frequency dropdown), Apply Withdrawal Fee for Transfers checkbox, Balance Required for Interest Calculation, Enforce Minimum Balance checkbox with Minimum Balance field, Is Overdraft Allowed checkbox with Maximum Overdraft Amount and Nominal Annual Interest for Overdraft fields, Enable Withhold Tax checkbox with Tax Group dropdown, and Enable Dormancy Tracking with Days to Inactive, Days to Dormant, and Days to Escheat fields. Charges — Add predefined charges with a search-and-add interface. Accounting — radio buttons for None or Cash-based with GL account mappings for Savings Reference, Savings Control, Transfers in Suspense, Interest on Savings, Income from Fees, Income from Penalties, and Escheat Liability. Fixed Deposit Products include additional fields for Minimum/Maximum/Default Deposit Amount, Interest Rate Chart with tiered rates based on deposit amount and term (Period From/To with corresponding interest rates and Amount Range slabs), and Pre-closure settings (Pre-Mature Closure Allowed checkbox, Pre-Closure Penal Interest rate). Recurring Deposit Products include similar additional fields with mandatory deposit amounts and frequency settings. "Submit" creates the product.
Functional Overview.9. Share Products: The Share Products page is accessed from the sidebar under the Products section. The page displays a data table listing all configured share products with columns for Product Name (clickable link), Short Name, and Total Shares. A "+" FAB opens the Create Share Product form with sections: Details — Product Name (text input, required), Short Name (text input, required, max 4 characters), Description (textarea), Currency (dropdown with decimal places and multiples of rounding), Total Number of Shares (numeric input, required, defines the total shares available for issuance), Shares to be Issued (numeric input), Nominal/Unit Price (numeric input, required), and Capital Value. Settings — Share Type (Equity), Allow Dividends for Inactive Clients (checkbox), Minimum Shares per Client (numeric input), Maximum Shares per Client (numeric input), Nominal Shares per Client (default allocation), Minimum Active Period Frequency (numeric input and dropdown — Days, Months), and Lock-in Period (numeric input and dropdown). Market Price — a table defining market price periods with From Date, To Date, and Share Price columns for tracking historical price changes. Charges — add predefined share charges with a search-and-add interface (Activation Fee, Purchase Fee, Redemption Fee). Accounting — radio buttons for None or Cash-based with GL account mappings for Share Reference, Share Suspense, Equity in Shares, Income from Fees, and Share Equity. All required fields show inline validation errors. "Submit" creates the share product. Clicking an existing product opens its detail view with Edit and Delete options.
Functional Overview.10. Charges: The Charges page is accessed from the sidebar under the Products section. The page displays a data table of all charge definitions with columns for Charge Name (clickable link), Charge Applies To (Loans, Savings, Client, Shares), Charge Type (Fee, Penalty), and Amount. A "+" FAB opens the Create Charge form with fields for Charge Name (text input, required), Charge Applies To (dropdown — Loan, Savings Account, Client, Shares, required), Currency (dropdown, required), Charge Time Type (dropdown — varies by entity: for Loans includes Disbursement, Specified Due Date, Installment Fee, Overdue Fees, Tranche Disbursement; for Savings includes Specified Due Date, Savings Activation, Withdrawal Fee, Annual Fee, Monthly Fee, Overdraft Fee), Charge Calculation Type (dropdown — Flat, Percentage of Amount, Percentage of Interest, Percentage of Total Outstanding), Amount (numeric input, required), Is Penalty (checkbox), Is Active (checkbox), Tax Group (dropdown, optional), and Payment Mode (dropdown — Regular, Account Transfer). All required fields show inline validation errors if empty. "Submit" creates the charge definition which can then be associated with loan or savings products. Clicking an existing charge name opens its detail view with Edit and Delete options.
Functional Overview.11. Floating Rates: The Floating Rates page is accessed from the sidebar under the Products section. The page displays a data table listing all configured floating rate definitions with columns for Floating Rate Name (clickable link), Is Base Lending Rate indicator, Is Active indicator, and Created By. A "+" FAB opens the Create Floating Rate form with fields for Floating Rate Name (text input, required), Is Base Lending Rate (checkbox — only one base rate can exist in the system), and Is Active (checkbox). The Rate Periods section allows defining time-segmented interest rates — each row has From Date (date picker), Interest Rate (numeric input, percentage), and Is Differential Rate checkbox (if checked, the rate is added to or subtracted from the base lending rate rather than used as an absolute value). Multiple rate periods can be added to define rate changes over time. "Submit" creates the floating rate definition. Floating rates are referenced by Loan Products when configuring variable interest rates — when a loan product is linked to a floating rate, the loan's interest rate adjusts automatically based on the applicable rate period. The detail view shows the full rate history with Edit option to add new periods or modify existing ones.
Functional Overview.12. Delinquency Management: The Delinquency Buckets page is accessed from the sidebar under the Products section or the Admin section (depending on deployment configuration). The page displays a data table of all delinquency range definitions with columns for Range Name (clickable link) and Classification. A "+" FAB opens the Create Delinquency Range form with fields for Classification (text input, required, e.g., "NO_DELINQUENCY", "RANGE_1", "RANGE_30", "RANGE_60"), Minimum Age Days (numeric input, required — minimum days past due), and Maximum Age Days (numeric input, optional — maximum days past due; if blank, applies to all days beyond minimum). Delinquency Buckets group these ranges into ordered sets. The Create Delinquency Bucket form takes a Bucket Name (text input, required) and allows adding multiple delinquency ranges in sequence (e.g., 1–29 days = Range 1, 30–59 days = Range 2, 60–89 days = Range 3, 90+ days = Range 4). Delinquency buckets are linked to Loan Products in the product configuration — when a loan becomes overdue, the system automatically classifies it into the appropriate delinquency range based on the number of days past due. This classification is used for portfolio at risk reporting, provisioning calculations, and collection workflow triggers. Clicking an existing bucket or range opens its detail view with Edit and Delete options.
Functional Overview.13. Loan Account: A new loan application is initiated from the Client Detail page by clicking the "+" FAB and selecting "New Loan". The Loan Application form is a multi-step wizard. Step 1 — Details: Product Name (dropdown listing all active loan products — selecting a product auto-populates default values), Loan Officer (dropdown), Loan Purpose (dropdown from configured code values), Fund (dropdown), Submitted On date (date picker), Expected Disbursement Date (date picker), Principal (numeric input, bounded by product min/max), Number of Repayments (numeric input), Repaid Every (frequency and unit), Interest Rate (numeric input, bounded by product min/max), and External ID. Step 2 — Terms: Repayment strategy, Amortization, Interest Method, and Interest Calculation Period dropdowns pre-filled from the product but adjustable, plus various grace period fields. Step 3 — Charges: A table listing charges inherited from the product with amount, charge time, and calculation type, plus an "Add Charge" button for additional charges. Step 4 — Collateral (optional): Add collateral items with Collateral Type, Value, and Description. "Submit" creates the loan in "Submitted and Pending Approval" status. The Loan Detail page header displays the loan account number, product name, client name (clickable link), loan status badge (Submitted and Pending Approval in yellow, Approved in blue, Active in green, Closed in gray, Overpaid in purple, Written Off in red), and loan balance. An action button bar provides lifecycle transition buttons based on status: for Pending Approval — Approve (opens dialog with Approved On Date, Approved Amount, Expected Disbursement Date, and Note fields), Reject, Withdraw, Delete; for Approved — Disburse (opens form with Disbursed On Date, Transaction Amount, Payment Type dropdown listing Cash/Check/Mobile Money/Bank Transfer, Account Number, Check Number, Receipt Number, and Bank Number fields; a "Disburse to Savings" option deposits directly into the client's linked savings), Undo Approval; for Active — Make Repayment (opens form with Transaction Date, Transaction Amount pre-filled with amount due, Payment Type dropdown, and payment detail fields; partial repayments are applied per the configured repayment strategy; full settlement changes status to "Closed obligations met"), Waive Interest, Write Off (records the total outstanding as a loss and closes the loan), Close, Reschedule (opens request form with Reschedule From Date, Reason, Adjusted Due Date, Grace on Principal/Interest, Extra Terms, and New Interest Rate — creates a pending request requiring separate approval), Prepay Loan, Foreclosure, Charge Off, Assign Loan Officer. The main content tabs include: Summary (disbursement details, repayment schedule summary, total paid, total outstanding, total overdue, charges summary), Repayment Schedule (table with columns for Installment Number, Due Date, Principal Due, Interest Due, Fees Due, Penalties Due, Total Due, amounts paid for each, Total Outstanding, and status indicators), Transactions (table of all loan transactions with Date, Type, Amount, Principal/Interest/Fees/Penalties portions, Outstanding Balance, and details/undo link), Charges (applied charges with charge name, amount, due date, paid, waived, outstanding), Collateral, Notes, and Documents.
Functional Overview.14. Savings Account: A new savings account is created from the Client Detail page by clicking the "+" FAB and selecting "New Savings". The form includes Product Name (dropdown listing all active savings products — selecting a product auto-populates default values), Field Officer (dropdown), Submitted On date (date picker), Nominal Annual Interest Rate (numeric input, pre-filled from product), Interest Compounding Period, Interest Posting Period, Interest Calculated Using, Days in Year dropdowns (all pre-filled), Minimum Opening Balance, Lock-in Period, and Allow Overdraft checkbox. A Charges section lists product charges and allows adding additional charges. "Submit" creates the account in "Submitted and Pending Approval" status. The Savings Account Detail page displays the account number, product name, client name, status badge (Submitted and Pending Approval, Approved, Active, Dormant, Closed, Blocked), account balance, and available balance. Action buttons change based on status: for Pending — Approve, Reject, Withdraw Application; for Approved — Activate, Undo Approval; for Active — Deposit (opens form with Transaction Date, Transaction Amount, Payment Type dropdown with Cash/Check/Mobile Money options, Account Number, Check Number, Routing Code, Receipt Number, Bank Number, and Note fields; submitting credits the savings account), Withdraw (same form as Deposit; system validates withdrawal does not exceed available balance unless overdraft is enabled; if minimum balance is enforced and withdrawal would breach it, an error is shown), Post Interest, Calculate Interest, Close, Block Account, Block Debit, Block Credit. Tabs show: Summary (account details and interest summary), Transactions (table of all deposits, withdrawals, and interest postings with date, type, amount, and running balance), Charges (applied charges with status), and Documents.
Functional Overview.15. Share Account: A new share account is created from the Client Detail page by clicking the "+" FAB and selecting "New Share Account". The Share Account Application form includes Share Product (dropdown listing all active share products — selecting a product auto-populates default values), Submitted On date (date picker), Requested Shares (numeric input — number of shares the client wishes to purchase, bounded by product min/max per client), Application Date (date picker), Savings Account for Charges (dropdown listing the client's active savings accounts to debit fees from), and External ID. A Charges section lists product-inherited charges and allows adding additional charges. "Submit" creates the share account in "Submitted and Pending Approval" status. The Share Account Detail page displays the share account number, product name, client name, status badge (Submitted and Pending Approval, Approved, Active, Closed, Rejected), total approved shares, total pending shares, and unit price. Action buttons change based on status: for Pending — Approve (with Approved Shares and Approved Date), Reject; for Approved — Activate, Undo Approval; for Active — Apply Additional Shares (purchase more shares), Redeem Shares (sell back shares — the redemption amount is calculated as shares multiplied by current unit price and credited to the linked savings account), Close. Tabs show: Purchased Shares (table of all purchase and redemption transactions with Date, Type, Number of Shares, Unit Price, Amount, and Status), Dividends (posted dividend distributions with date, amount per share, and total amount), and Charges.
Functional Overview.16. Fixed & Recurring Deposit Accounts: Fixed Deposit (FD) and Recurring Deposit (RD) accounts are created from the Client Detail page. The FD Account creation form includes Fixed Deposit Product (dropdown), Deposit Amount (numeric input), Deposit Period (numeric input and unit dropdown — Days, Months, Years), and maturity instructions (Transfer to Savings, Re-Invest, or Re-Invest Principal and Transfer Interest). The FD Account Detail page shows the deposit amount, maturity date, maturity amount, interest rate, and status with action buttons for Approve, Activate, Premature Close, and Close on Maturity. The RD Account creation form includes Recurring Deposit Product (dropdown), Mandatory Deposit Amount (numeric input per installment), Deposit Period, Deposit Frequency (dropdown — Daily, Weekly, Monthly, Quarterly, Yearly), and Expected First Deposit On date. The RD Account Detail page shows the deposit schedule, total deposits made, maturity details, interest rate, and status with action buttons for Approve, Activate, Deposit (to make recurring deposits), Premature Close, and Close on Maturity. Both FD and RD detail pages include tabs for Summary (account and interest details), Transactions (table of deposit and interest posting transactions), and Charges. Interest rates are determined by the Interest Rate Chart configured on the product, with tiered rates based on deposit amount and term length.
Functional Overview.17. Accounting - Chart of Accounts: The Chart of Accounts page is accessed from the sidebar under the Accounting section. The page displays a hierarchical tree view or table of all General Ledger (GL) accounts organized by account type: Assets, Liabilities, Equity, Income, and Expenses. Each row shows the GL Code, Account Name, Account Type (with color coding — Assets in blue, Liabilities in red, Equity in green, Income in purple, Expenses in orange), Manual Entries Allowed indicator, Usage (Header or Detail), and Description. Header accounts serve as grouping parents while Detail accounts are used for actual journal postings. A "+" FAB opens the Create GL Account form with fields for Account Type (dropdown — Asset, Liability, Equity, Income, Expense, required), Parent Account (dropdown listing header accounts of the same type), GL Code (text input, required, must be unique), Account Name (text input, required), Account Usage (radio buttons — Header, Detail), Manual Entries Allowed (checkbox), Description (textarea), and Tag (dropdown). Required fields show validation errors if empty (e.g., "GL Code is required", "Account Name is required"). If a duplicate GL Code is entered, an error "GL Code already exists" is displayed. "Submit" creates the GL account. Clicking an account name opens its detail view with Edit and Delete options.
Functional Overview.18. Accounting - Journal Entries & Closu: The Journal Entries page is accessed from the sidebar under the Accounting section. The page displays a data table of recent journal entries with columns for Entry ID, Office, Transaction Date, Created Date, Transaction ID, Type (Debit/Credit), GL Account, and Amount. A filter bar at the top allows filtering by Office (dropdown), GL Account (dropdown), Date Range (from/to date pickers), Transaction ID (text input), and Entry Type. A "+" FAB or "Add Journal Entry" button opens the Create Journal Entry form with fields for Office (dropdown, required), Currency (dropdown, required), Reference Number (text input), Transaction Date (date picker, required), Payment Type (dropdown), and payment detail fields (Account Number, Check Number, Receipt Number, Routing Code, Bank Number). The entry lines section allows adding multiple debit and credit lines: each line has a GL Account (dropdown with search/autocomplete) and Amount (numeric input). An "Add Row" button adds additional lines. A running total display shows the debit total, credit total, and difference — the total debits must equal total credits for the entry to balance. A Comments field (textarea) allows adding a description. If totals don't balance, the validation error "The debits and credits must be equal" prevents submission. "Submit" posts the journal entry. The Closing Entries page (also under Accounting) displays a table of existing closures with columns for Office, Closing Date, Created Date, and Comments. A "+" button opens the Create Closure form with fields for Office (dropdown, required), Closing Date (date picker, required), and Comments (textarea). Creating a closure prevents journal entries from being posted for dates on or before the closing date, ensuring the integrity of completed accounting periods. The Accounting Rules page allows creating automated posting rules mapping an office, debit account(s), and credit account(s) for common transaction types.
Functional Overview.19. Accounting Rules & Financial Activit: The Accounting Rules page is accessed from the sidebar under the Accounting section. The page displays a data table of all configured accounting rules with columns for Rule Name (clickable link), Office, Debit Account(s), and Credit Account(s). A "+" FAB opens the Create Accounting Rule form with fields for Office (dropdown — specifies which office the rule applies to, or blank for all offices), Rule Name (text input, required), Debit Tags / Debit Account (dropdown or multi-select listing GL accounts to debit), Allow Multiple Debit Entries (checkbox), Credit Tags / Credit Account (dropdown or multi-select listing GL accounts to credit), and Allow Multiple Credit Entries (checkbox). Accounting rules automate journal entry creation for certain transaction types by pre-defining the debit and credit GL account mappings. "Submit" creates the rule. Clicking a rule opens its detail view with Edit and Delete options. The Financial Activity Mappings page (also under Accounting) displays a table mapping financial activities to GL accounts. Each row shows the Financial Activity (e.g., Asset Transfer, Liability Transfer, Cash at Mainvault, Cash at Teller, Opening Balances Transfer, Fund Source, Payable Dividends, Asset Fund Source, Overdraft Portfolio) and the mapped GL Account. A "+" button opens the Create Mapping form with Financial Activity (dropdown listing all unmapped activities) and GL Account (dropdown). Each financial activity can only be mapped once. These mappings enable the system to automatically post accounting entries for internal transfers, teller operations, and other system-level financial activities.
Functional Overview.20. Provisioning: The Provisioning pages are accessed from the sidebar under the Accounting section. The Provisioning Criteria page displays a data table of all defined provisioning criteria with columns for Criteria Name (clickable link) and Created Date. A "+" FAB opens the Create Provisioning Criteria form with Criteria Name (text input, required) and a definitions table where each row specifies: Loan Product (dropdown), Category (dropdown — e.g., STANDARD, SUB-STANDARD, DOUBTFUL, LOSS based on delinquency classification), Minimum Age (numeric input — minimum overdue days), Maximum Age (numeric input — maximum overdue days), Provisioning Percentage (numeric input — the percentage of outstanding principal to provision), Liability Account (GL account dropdown for the provisioning liability), and Expense Account (GL account dropdown for the provisioning expense). Multiple rows can be added to cover different overdue ranges and product combinations. "Submit" creates the criteria. The Provisioning Entries page lists all generated provisioning journal entries with columns for Entry Date, Journal Entry Created, Created By, and actions. A "+" or "Create Provisioning Entry" button generates new provisioning entries based on the current loan portfolio status and configured criteria — the system calculates the required provision amount for each loan category and creates the corresponding journal entries. Each entry can be reviewed (showing the detailed breakdown by loan product and category) or recreated. Provisioning is essential for regulatory compliance, ensuring financial institutions maintain adequate reserves against potential loan losses.
Functional Overview.21. Offices: The Offices page is accessed from the sidebar under the Organization section. The page displays a hierarchical tree view or table of all offices showing Office Name (clickable link), External ID, Parent Office, Opened Date, and status. A "+" FAB opens the Create Office form with fields for Office Name (text input, required), Parent Office (dropdown listing existing offices for hierarchy, required — Head Office is the root), Opened On Date (date picker, required), and External ID (text input, optional). Required fields show validation errors if empty. Upon submission, the new office is created under the selected parent in the organizational hierarchy. The Office Detail page shows the office information and provides Edit option. Offices define the branch structure — clients, groups, staff, and accounting operations are all associated with specific offices.
Functional Overview.22. Employees: The Employees page is accessed from the sidebar under the Organization section. The page displays a data table of all staff members with columns for Name (clickable link), Office, Is Loan Officer indicator, and Status (Active, Inactive). A "+" FAB opens the Create Employee form with fields for Office (dropdown, required), First Name (text input, required), Last Name (text input, required), Is Loan Officer (checkbox), Mobile Number (text input), Is Active (checkbox, defaults to true), Joining Date (date picker), and External ID (text input). Required fields show validation errors if empty. "Submit" creates the staff record. Staff members marked as "Is Loan Officer" appear in the Loan Officer dropdown when creating loans and can be assigned to clients, groups, and centers for portfolio management. The Staff Detail page shows the employee's information with an Edit option.
Functional Overview.23. Teller & Cashier Management: The Teller / Cashier Management page is accessed from the sidebar under the Organization section. The Tellers page displays a data table of all configured tellers with columns for Teller Name (clickable link), Office, Status (Active, Inactive), and Description. A "+" FAB opens the Create Teller form with fields for Office (dropdown, required), Teller Name (text input, required), Description (textarea), Start Date (date picker, required), End Date (date picker, optional), and Status (dropdown — Active, Inactive). "Submit" creates the teller. The Teller Detail page displays teller information and provides an Edit option. Below the teller details, a Cashiers section lists all cashier assignments for that teller with columns for Cashier Name, Start Date, End Date, Is Full Day, and Status. A "+" button opens the Allocate Cashier form with fields for Staff (dropdown listing employees, required), Start Date (date picker, required), End Date (date picker), Is Full Day (checkbox), and Description. Cashier allocation links a staff member to a teller terminal for a specified period. The Cashier Detail page shows the allocated cash — including Opening Balance, Cash In Hand, and transactions. Actions include Allocate Cash (add cash to the teller from the vault), Settle Cash (return cash from the teller to the vault — with Amount, Currency, and Transaction Date fields), and view the Cashier Transactions list showing all allocations, settlements, deposits, and withdrawals processed through that teller with Date, Type, Amount, and Running Balance columns.
Functional Overview.24. Users & Roles: The Users page is accessed from the sidebar under the Admin section. The page displays a data table of all system users with columns for Username (clickable link), First Name, Last Name, Email, Office, and Status. A "+" FAB opens the Create User form with fields for Username (text input, required, must be unique), First Name (text input, required), Last Name (text input, required), Email (text input, required, validated for email format), Office (dropdown, required), Staff (dropdown to link user to a staff record), Password (password input, required, must meet configured password policy), Repeat Password (must match), Roles (multi-select checkboxes listing all available roles), and Overrides Password Expiration checkbox. If the username already exists, an error "Username already exists" is displayed. If passwords don't match, "Passwords do not match" is shown. "Submit" creates the user. The Roles page (also under Admin) displays all roles with Name and Description columns. Creating a role involves entering a Role Name and Description, then configuring granular permissions — the permissions page displays a categorized matrix of all system permissions (User Management, Portfolio, Organization, Accounting, Reports, etc.) with checkboxes for each permission. Enabling or disabling specific permissions controls what sidebar menu items, pages, and actions the role can access.
Functional Overview.25. Reports: The Reports section is accessed from the sidebar. The page lists available reports grouped by category: Client Reports, Loan Reports, Savings Reports, Fund Reports, and Accounting Reports. Each report name is a clickable link. Clicking a report opens a parameters form tailored to that report type, with fields such as Office (dropdown), Branch (dropdown), Currency (dropdown), Loan Product (dropdown), Date Range (From Date and To Date pickers), Loan Officer (dropdown), Fund (dropdown), and other report-specific parameters. Required parameter fields show validation errors if empty. A "Run Report" button generates the report which displays as a data table in the main content area with sorting capabilities. Report output options include viewing in a table format on screen, exporting to Excel (XLS/XLSX), exporting to CSV, and exporting to PDF. Common reports include Client Listing, Loan Portfolio Summary, Aging Summary, Portfolio at Risk, Collection Report, Trial Balance, Income Statement, Balance Sheet, Journal Entry Report, Expected vs Actual Payments, Loan Disbursement Report, and Savings Account Summary.
Functional Overview.26. Account Transfers & Standing Instruc: The Account Transfers function is accessible from the Savings Account Detail page or from a Standing Instructions management page in the sidebar. The transfer form contains fields for From Account Type (dropdown — Savings Account, Loan Account), From Account (dropdown listing the client's accounts), To Account Type (dropdown — Savings Account, Loan Account), To Account (dropdown listing target accounts — can be same client or different clients), Transfer Amount (numeric input, required), Transfer Date (date picker, required), and Description (textarea). If the transfer amount exceeds the available balance, an error "Insufficient balance" is shown. "Submit" processes the transfer, debiting the source and crediting the destination. The Standing Instructions page displays a data table of all configured recurring transfers with columns for Instruction Name, From Account, To Account, Amount, Frequency, Status (Active, Disabled), and actions. A "+" button opens the Create Standing Instruction form with fields for From Account, To Account, Amount (or entire balance option), Transfer Frequency (dropdown — Daily, Weekly, Monthly, Quarterly, Yearly), Start Date, End Date, Priority, and Instruction Type (Fixed Amount, Account Balance). Standing instructions can be enabled, disabled, or deleted from the listing.
Functional Overview.27. Tax Management: The Tax Management pages are accessed from the sidebar under the Products section or the Organization section (depending on deployment configuration). The Tax Components page displays a data table of all tax components with columns for Component Name (clickable link), Percentage, Debit Account, Credit Account, and Start Date. A "+" FAB opens the Create Tax Component form with fields for Tax Component Name (text input, required), Percentage (numeric input, required — the tax rate to apply), Debit Account Type (dropdown — Asset, Liability, Equity, Income, Expense) and Debit Account (GL account dropdown), Credit Account Type (dropdown) and Credit Account (GL account dropdown), and Start Date (date picker, required). "Submit" creates the tax component. The Tax Groups page displays a data table of tax groups with columns for Group Name (clickable link) and associated components. A "+" FAB opens the Create Tax Group form with Tax Group Name (text input, required) and a Tax Components section allowing multiple tax components to be added with Start Date and End Date for each. Tax groups combine multiple tax components into a single applicable rate. Tax groups are referenced by Savings Products (Enable Withhold Tax checkbox with Tax Group dropdown) and Charge definitions — when a product has tax enabled, the system automatically applies the configured tax rate on interest income or fees and posts the corresponding journal entries to the mapped GL accounts.
Functional Overview.28. Organization Settings: The Organization section in the sidebar provides access to several configuration pages. The Holidays page displays a table of configured holidays with columns for Holiday Name, Start Date, End Date, Status (Active, Pending), and Repayment Rescheduling Type. A "+" button opens the Create Holiday form with fields for Holiday Name (text input, required), Start Date (date picker, required), End Date (date picker, required), Repayment Rescheduling Type (dropdown — Reschedule to next repayment date, Move to next working day), Description (textarea), and applicable Offices (multi-select). Holidays affect loan repayment schedules — installments falling on holidays are automatically rescheduled per the configured type. The Working Days page allows configuring which days of the week are working days using checkboxes for Monday through Sunday and a Repayment Rescheduling dropdown for handling repayments on non-working days. The Currencies page allows selecting active currencies from available currencies using an add/remove interface, each showing Code, Name, and Decimal Places. The Funds page manages funding sources for loans with a table listing Fund Name and External ID, and a Create Fund form with Fund Name (required) and External ID fields. The Payment Types page lists all configured payment methods (Cash, Bank Transfer, Mobile Money, Check, Receipt) with Name, Description, Is Cash Payment indicator, and Position columns; a "+" button creates new payment types which appear as dropdown options in all transaction forms across the system.
Functional Overview.29. System Administration: The Admin section in the sidebar provides access to several system management pages. The Manage Scheduler Jobs page displays a table of all batch jobs with columns for Job Name, Is Active (toggle), CRON Expression, Previous Run Status (Success/Failed), Next Run Time, and action buttons. Jobs include Apply Annual Fee, Add Accrual Transactions, Apply Penalty to Overdue Loans, Update Loan Summary, Post Interest for Savings, Transfer Fees, Generate Loan Loss Provisioning, and others. Each job can be toggled active/inactive and its CRON expression edited. A global scheduler toggle (Start/Stop) controls whether scheduled jobs execute. The Global Configuration page displays a table of system-wide feature flags with columns for Configuration Name, Enabled (toggle), and Value. Key settings include maker-checker-enabled (4-eyes approval), reschedule-future-repayments, penalty-wait-period, savings-interest-posting-current-period-end, min/max-clients-in-group, and meeting-mandatory-for-jlg-loans. The Manage Codes page lists all code lists (Client Type, Gender, Address Type, Loan Purpose, etc.) with Name and Is System Defined columns; clicking a code opens its values for adding, editing, reordering, and deactivating entries that populate dropdown options throughout the system. The Manage Data Tables page lists custom data tables that extend core entities (Clients, Loans, Savings, Groups) with custom fields; the Create form includes Table Name, Application Table (dropdown), and column definitions (Name, Type, Length, Is Mandatory, Is Unique). The Audit Trails page displays all system actions with columns for Action Name, Entity, Resource ID, Maker, Checker, Date, and Status (Pending, Approved, Rejected); filters allow searching by Action, Entity, User, and Date Range; when maker-checker is enabled, pending commands appear with Approve/Reject buttons. The Bulk Import page allows importing data via Excel templates by selecting an entity type (Clients, Loans, Savings, etc.), downloading a pre-formatted template, filling in data, uploading the file, reviewing validation results showing success/error counts, and submitting valid records for processing.
Functional Overview.Logout: The logout function is accessible from the user menu dropdown in the top-right corner of the toolbar. Clicking the user's name reveals a dropdown menu with options for Profile Settings and Log Out. Clicking Log Out immediately terminates the authenticated session, clears the authentication token stored in the browser, and redirects the user to the login page with empty Tenant, Username, and Password fields. After logout, any attempt to navigate to an authenticated page redirects to the login page. The system ensures no sensitive financial data, client information, or session tokens remain accessible after the session ends.
